arguments.task: Research Plaid using official first-party sources only where possible. Open https://plaid.com/docs and find pages that document: API authentication methods, getting API keys or tokens, available API surface/endpoints, rate limits, pricing or access requirements for developers, and whether Plaid offers an MCP server. Also check first-party Plaid pages such as docs, API reference, pricing, dashboard/developer/get-started, changelog, and GitHub pages if linked from first-party docs. Return a concise list of exact source URLs with supporting snippets for each topic.

input.thought: Launch a focused browser research task on official Plaid documentation and first-party pages.

raw_result.description: Financial data network API for connecting bank accounts and accessing account, identity, balance, transaction, and payment data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Plaid docs indicate requests are authenticated with a client_id and secret included in the JSON body for POST requests; product flows also use Link/public_token and exchanged access_token patterns, so auth is not pure standard OAuth for API access.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developers can sign up via the Plaid Dashboard and test in Sandbox for free; pricing page also states Limited Production includes up to 200 live API calls for free before paid tiers.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API covering products such as Auth, Balance, Identity, Signal, Transfer, Transactions, Investments, and Liabilities with many documented endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP server was found in Plaid docs or Plaid's GitHub organization from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate limits are endpoint- and environment-specific rather than a single global limit. Gathered examples from Plaid's rate-limit error docs include /accounts/balance/get at 5 requests/minute per Item in Production and /identity/get at 15 requests/minute per Item plus 2,000/minute per client.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are public and detailed; self-serve signup exists; pricing includes free Sandbox and limited free live usage; Plaid maintains an official GitHub org; auth uses Plaid-specific credentials/token exchange rather than OAuth2 for core API calls.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Auth classification is slightly ambiguous because Plaid uses client_id/secret plus Plaid-specific token exchange flows rather than a standard Authorization header scheme, and MCP absence is based on negative evidence from gathered docs/GitHub rather than an explicit statement.
